beats.text: You learned to solve problems that need two steps. You know to find the combined total first, then compare. Let's see what you know.

incorrects.description: Student selected 60 (added pencils and markers instead of finding the difference)

params.description: Pencils bar (40) and Markers bar (20) highlight.

steps.text: When we ask how many more, we compare. That means subtract, not add. Pencils is 40, markers is 20. What's the difference?

incorrects.description: Student selected 40 (chose the value for pencils without comparing)

params.description: Pencils bar (40) and Markers bar (20) highlight.

steps.text: That's how many pencils were sold. But the question asks how many MORE. We need to find the difference. Pencils is 40, markers is 20. Subtract to compare.

incorrects.description: Student selected 15 (incorrect calculation or misread values)

params.description: Pencils bar (40) and Markers bar (20) highlight.

steps.text: Check the values carefully. Pencils is 40, markers is 20. When we compare, we subtract: 40 minus 20.

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: Animation highlights Pencils (40) and Markers (20), then shows 40 minus 20 equals 20.

steps.text: Let me show you. How many more means compare. Pencils is 40, markers is 20. We subtract: 40 minus 20 equals 20. The answer is 20 more pencils sold than markers. That's what compare means: find the difference.

incorrects.description: Student selected comparing fiction to nonfiction, skipping the combined step

params.description: Nonfiction and Poetry bars highlight together.

steps.text: The question asks about nonfiction and poetry combined. When you see combined, find that total first.

incorrects.description: Student selected adding all categories, not focusing on the needed categories

params.description: Nonfiction and Poetry bars highlight.

steps.text: We don't need all categories. The question only asks about nonfiction and poetry combined. Start with those two.

incorrects.description: Student selected subtracting poetry from nonfiction, incorrect operation for combined

params.description: Nonfiction and Poetry bars highlight together.

steps.text: Combined means add, not subtract. We need the total of nonfiction and poetry together.

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct first step.

params.description: Nonfiction and Poetry bars highlight. Calculation shows: 30 + 20 = 50.

steps.text: When we see combined, we add first. Nonfiction is 30, poetry is 20. That's 50 combined. The answer is to add nonfiction and poetry.

incorrects.description: Student selected 10, subtracted values instead of adding

params.description: Nonfiction and Poetry bars pulse to emphasize adding.

steps.text: Combined means add them together, not subtract. Nonfiction is 30, poetry is 20. What's 30 plus 20?

incorrects.description: Student selected 40, may have confused with Comics value or miscounted

params.description: Nonfiction and Poetry bars highlight. Values 30 and 20 emphasized.

steps.text: Look at nonfiction: 30. Look at poetry: 20. Add those two together. What do you get?

incorrects.description: Student selected 60, may have added extra categories or misread values

params.description: Only Nonfiction and Poetry bars highlighted.

steps.text: We only need these two categories. Nonfiction is 30, poetry is 20. Count just those two bars.

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct calculation.

params.description: Nonfiction bar (30) and Poetry bar (20) highlight. Calculation appears: 30 + 20 = 50.

steps.text: Nonfiction is 30 books. Poetry is 20 books. When we add them, 30 plus 20 equals 50. The combined total is 50.

incorrects.description: Student selected 100, added instead of subtracting

params.description: Fiction bar highlights alongside displayed value 50.

steps.text: How many more means compare. Fiction is 50, combined is 50. Are they the same or different?

incorrects.description: Student selected 50, stated one value instead of finding the difference

params.description: Fiction bar highlights. Combined value 50 displayed next to it.

steps.text: 50 is the fiction value, but the question asks how many more. Subtract: 50 minus 50. What's the difference?

incorrects.description: Student selected 10, may have miscalculated or compared wrong categories

params.description: Fiction bar highlights. Combined value 50 displayed.

steps.text: Fiction is 50. Combined is 50. When two amounts are equal, the difference is zero, not 10.

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the comparison.

params.description: Fiction bar (50) and combined value (50) appear side by side. Calculation shows: 50 - 50 = 0.

steps.text: Fiction is 50 books. Nonfiction and poetry combined is also 50. When we compare equal amounts, 50 minus 50 equals 0. There is no difference. The answer is 0.

incorrects.description: Student selected 'Compare zoo to museum' (skipped the combine step)

params.description: Museum and Farm bars highlight.

steps.text: The question says museum and farm combined. That word combined tells us to add those two first.

incorrects.description: Student selected 'Add all five places' (added categories not mentioned in the question)

params.description: Museum and Farm bars highlight.

steps.text: We only need the categories the question asks about. It says museum and farm combined. Add those two.

incorrects.description: Student selected 'Subtract farm from museum' (wrong operation for combined)

params.description: Museum and Farm bars highlight.

steps.text: Combined means add, not subtract. When we see combined or together, we find the total first.

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: Animation shows Museum (25) and Farm (15) combining: 25 + 15 = 40

steps.text: Let me show you. The question says museum and farm combined. That word combined tells us to add first. Museum is 25, farm is 15. Combined, that's 40. The first step is to add museum and farm.

incorrects.description: Student selected 10 (subtracted instead of added)

params.description: Animation shows: 25 + 15 = 40

steps.text: Combined means add, not subtract. Museum is 25, farm is 15. Add them: 25 plus 15 equals what?

incorrects.description: Student selected 30 (miscounted or used wrong values)

params.description: Museum (25) and Farm (15) bars highlight.

steps.text: Check the bar heights. Museum goes to 25, farm goes to 15. Add those two numbers.

incorrects.description: Student selected 45 (zoo's value instead of the combined total)

params.description: Museum (25) and Farm (15) bars pulse.

steps.text: That's the zoo's votes. We need museum and farm combined. Add museum's 25 and farm's 15.

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: System demonstrates: Museum bar (25) and Farm bar (15) combine. 25 + 15 = 40 displays.

steps.text: Here's how. Museum is 25 votes. Farm is 15 votes. When we add them, 25 plus 15 equals 40. Museum and farm combined got 40 votes.

incorrects.description: Student selected 85 (added 45 + 40 instead of subtracting)

params.description: Zoo bar (45) highlights.

steps.text: How many fewer means compare. Compare means subtract. Zoo is 45, combined is 40. What's the difference?

incorrects.description: Student selected 45 (zoo's value, not the difference)

params.description: Zoo bar (45) and combined value 40 display.

steps.text: That's how many votes the zoo got. We need the difference between 45 and 40. Subtract to find how many fewer.

incorrects.description: Student selected 40 (combined total, not the difference)

params.description: Zoo bar (45) highlights against combined total 40.

steps.text: That's the combined total. Now compare it to the zoo. How many fewer means find the difference. Zoo is 45, combined is 40. Subtract.

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: System demonstrates comparison: Zoo bar (45) compared to combined total (40). Shows 45 minus 40 = 5.

steps.text: Let me show you the second step. Museum and farm combined is 40. Zoo is 45. How many fewer asks for the difference. We subtract: 45 minus 40 equals 5. Museum and farm combined got 5 fewer votes than the zoo.

incorrects.description: Student selected 5: subtracted instead of adding (14 - 9 = 5)

params.description: Maya and Ben bars highlight.

steps.text: The question says together. That means add Maya's 14 books and Ben's 9 books. What's 14 plus 9?

incorrects.description: Student selected 14: chose Maya's value only, didn't add Ben

params.description: Maya and Ben bars highlight.

steps.text: You found Maya. But the question asks for Maya AND Ben together. Add both amounts.

incorrects.description: Student selected 22: misread scale or miscounted

params.description: Maya and Ben bars highlight.

steps.text: Check the scale. Each mark counts by 2. Maya is at 14, Ben is at 9. Count carefully and add those values.

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: Animation demonstrates: Maya bar at 14, Ben bar at 9. 14 + 9 = 23.

steps.text: Let me show you. Maya read 14 books. I find that by counting up by 2s to where her bar stops. Ben read 9. Together means add: 14 plus 9 equals 23. The answer is 23 books together.

incorrects.description: Student selected 34: added all values instead of subtracting (23 + 11 = 34)

params.description: Carla bar highlights.

steps.text: How many MORE means compare. We subtract. Maya and Ben together is 23. Carla is 11. What's 23 minus 11?

incorrects.description: Student selected 23: gave the combined total without subtracting Carla

params.description: Carla bar highlights.

steps.text: That's the combined total. But the question asks how many MORE than Carla. Compare 23 to Carla's 11. Subtract.

incorrects.description: Student selected 17: used incorrect combined total or subtraction error

params.description: Maya, Ben, and Carla bars highlight.

steps.text: Check your work. Maya and Ben together is 23. Carla is 11. To find how many more, subtract: 23 minus 11. What's that difference?

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: Animation demonstrates: combined total 23 displayed, Carla bar at 11 highlighted, calculation 23 - 11 = 12 shown.

steps.text: Here's how this works. Maya and Ben together read 23 books. Carla read 11. How many MORE means we compare by subtracting: 23 minus 11 equals 12. Maya and Ben together read 12 more books than Carla.
